metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a conflict-handling policy to AC1 (e.g. 'if a cross-stack rebase conflict is detected, exclude the conflicting change from the current batch and proceed with the remaining non-conflicting members; the excluded change waits for the next batch').
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the batch assembles 'N non-conflicting changes' but never specifies what action is taken when a conflict IS detected during cross-stack chain assembly: skip the conflicting change and continue assembling the rest, drop the entire batch, or fall back to serial immediately. An executing agent cannot proceed without choosing one of these strategies.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'Assembles queued changes/stacks into one combined linear chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'If a cross-stack rebase conflict is detected during assembly, remove the conflicting change from the current batch (it waits for the next batch cycle) and retry assembly with the remaining candidates — do not drop the entire batch on a single-member conflict.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that Gerrit's `POST /rebase:chain` supports cross-stack chain linearization (i.e., rebasing one independent stack's tip onto another stack's tip) or replace the AC1 building-block description with the actual API sequence required for cross-stack assembly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts AC1's 'reusing S2's `/rebase:chain` as the building block for cross-stack chain assembly' — but S2's own AC explicitly scopes `/rebase:chain` to single in-stack chains of >1 non-merge members. The claim that `/rebase:chain` is the correct building block for assembling *multiple independent stacks* into one linear chain is unverified against any code or Gerrit API documentation.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'reusing S2's `/rebase:chain` as the building block for cross-stack chain assembly'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how S7 prevents g2p from auto-dispatching individual `gerrit-verify.yaml` runs on the N newly-rebased member patchsets (e.g., a bot-owned 'skip-ci' marker on those patchsets, a Gerrit submit-rule that suppresses g2p dispatch for bot-assembled patchsets, or explicitly accepting that N extra runs fire and adjusting the 'exactly one' claim to 'one canonical run on the combined tip, with member runs non-binding').
findings.finding: S7 claims to trigger 'exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip,' but assembling the combined chain necessarily creates new patchsets on all N member changes via rebase (reuse of S2's rebase:chain). Each new patchset fires a g2p `patchset-created` event, which unconditionally dispatches `gerrit-verify.yaml` on each member's Change-Id (confirmed: gerrit_to_platform.ini.template — dispatch is branch-agnostic, unconditional on patchset-created; no suppression mechanism exists). This produces N g2p-triggered runs in addition to S7's single combined-tip dispatch, wasting GHA capacity and potentially casting stale intermediate Verified votes on non-tip member patchsets — undermining the 'exactly one run' invariant the plan asserts.
findings.location: AC (bullet 2): 'Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip (not one per change)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mechanism by which non-tip member patchset-created g2p dispatches are suppressed or cancelled during batch chain assembly (e.g., g2p event filtering, a WIP flag on intermediate changes, or an explicit cancel-workflow call after assembly completes), since g2p fires unconditionally on every patchset-created and the per-Change-Id concurrency group does not cross-cancel across members.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip (not one per change)' but the existing g2p hook fires workflow_dispatch unconditionally on every patchset-created event (confirmed: gerrit_to_platform.ini.template states 'unconditional find_and_dispatch(project,"verify",inputs)'). When the batch assembler rebases N changes into a combined chain, each member's new patchset triggers its own g2p dispatch against that member's Change-Id; because gerrit-verify.yaml's concurrency group is keyed per-Change-Id ('gerrit-verify-${GERRIT_CHANGE_ID}'), N−1 non-tip CI runs are not cancelled by the tip's run. No suppression mechanism is specified, leaving the 'exactly ONE CI run' claim unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip (not one per change)')
findings.suggested_fix: Mark all N−1 non-tip member changes as WIP ('work in progress') in Gerrit before initiating rebases; g2p's patchset-created hook does not dispatch for WIP changes (Gerrit suppresses hooks for WIP patchsets in its default hook configuration). Alternatively, document an explicit post-assembly step that cancels the N−1 spurious GHA runs via the Actions API before recording the tip's run_id.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a co-located decision rule to AC2 (or the batch record schema) specifying exactly how `combined_tip_change_id` is identified after cross-stack assembly (e.g. 'the topmost change in the linear chain after all /rebase:chain calls complete, i.e. the change with no child in the assembled order').
findings.finding: AC2 references 'the real combined-tip change's actual Change-Id / change-number / patchset' as the dispatch target, but never defines a decision rule for identifying *which* change in the assembled linear chain is the 'combined tip'. The schema names `combined_tip_change_id` but provides no protocol for how it is selected (topmost rebase result? last member in FIFO order? new synthetic change?) — the decision rule is absent from the schema definition.
findings.location: AC2 — CI trigger dispatch target; Verified-satisfaction section — handoff record

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the negative NOTE in AC3 with a positive Phase-2 concurrency invariant (e.g. 'Phase 2 holds at most one in-flight batch at a time; a second batch may not begin assembly until the first reaches ci_green or is dropped') to give implementers a concrete rule.
findings.finding: AC3 contains a pink-elephant/negative instruction: 'NOTE: the Phase-1 single-`wipChain` invariant does NOT hold in Phase 2 — this bounded re-batch loop is the mitigation.' This names the absent constraint but does not replace it with a positive concurrency/serialization invariant for Phase 2. The replacement protocol — what DOES bound concurrent batch assembly in Phase 2 — is unspecified.
findings.location: AC3 — FFO TOCTOU re-check, NOTE clause

findings.finding: The plan never specifies how the Verified gate is satisfied for non-tip chain members after assembly. Under the existing `copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE` (project.config line 152 / ADR-0040), rebasing any change onto the combined chain mints a TRIVIAL_REBASE or larger patchset — which drops the Verified vote. 'Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip' means only the tip change receives a new Verified signal; every non-tip member is left with Verified=0, failing the submit-requirement `label:Verified=MAX`. The plan does not describe how the bot casts Verified on non-tip members, or how the gate integrity holds — making the core mechanism mechanically incomplete as written.
findings.location: AC2 ('Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip') and AC3 ('On green, submits the whole batch atomically')
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'The bot casts Verified +1 on every member of the combined chain (not just the tip) using the single combined-tree CI result; the vote-per-member ACL is pre-authorized; the gate-integrity argument (each member's diff was tested as part of the combined tree) is recorded in an ADR amendment.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the g2p co-dispatch race: either suppress g2p dispatch for autolander-assembled patchsets (e.g. a special hashtag/topic that g2p is configured to skip), or rely on the concurrency group to cancel g2p's run and have S7 poll for run completion by the combined-tip Change-Id rather than recording a run_id at dispatch time.
findings.finding: When S7 rebases the combined chain tip to create a new patchset, Gerrit's `patchset-created` hook fires, causing g2p to also `workflow_dispatch` `gerrit-verify.yaml` for that same Change-Id. Because `gerrit-verify.yaml` uses `concurrency.group = gerrit-verify-${GERRIT_CHANGE_ID}` with `cancel-in-progress: true`, the g2p-dispatched run and S7's directly-dispatched run will race on the same concurrency group, and one will be cancelled — violating S7's 'exactly ONE CI run' guarantee and making the run_id recorded in the handoff record unreliable.
findings.location: AC2: 'Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip (not one per change)'; gerrit-verify.yaml concurrency block

findings.finding: The plan's AC2 ('Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip') directly contradicts the existing gerrit-to-platform dispatch model: gerrit-verify.yaml is triggered per patchset-created event, votes on a single GERRIT_CHANGE_NUMBER/GERRIT_PATCHSET_NUMBER, and uses a per-Change-Id concurrency group. A combined N-change batch would normally fire N independent g2p dispatches. No mechanism to suppress per-change g2p events for intermediate chain members, or to fan out a single combined Verified vote to all N changes, is named anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip (not one per change).'

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'assembles queued changes/stacks into ONE combined linear chain on the current main tip' (AC1) but names no Gerrit API endpoint or git operation for cross-stack, cross-uploader relation-chain assembly. Combining independently-uploaded changes (each with its own uploader identity, Change-Id, and possibly distinct topic) into a single Gerrit relation chain is a novel Gerrit operation not previously used in this codebase; its feasibility (especially with the FFO submit type and per-change ACL restrictions in project.config) is unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'Assembles queued changes/stacks into one combined linear chain on the current main tip.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/backoff (e.g., 3 attempts with exponential backoff) and a timeout for the GHA `gerrit-verify.yaml` dispatch call, with a defined fallback (drop batch to serial path or alert operator) if all retries are exhausted.
findings.finding: No error handling is specified for CI dispatch failure: if `gerrit-verify.yaml` dispatch to GHA fails (network error, GHA outage, rate-limit), the plan has no retry, timeout, circuit-breaker, or fallback defined. The batch would silently stall with no recovery path.
findings.location: AC line 2 — 'Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip… dispatching `gerrit-verify.yaml`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'If the CI dispatch call fails after N retries (e.g., 3) with exponential backoff, the batch is dropped and each member change returns to the Phase-1 serial queue; the failure is logged with structured fields (batch_id, error, attempt_count).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly which credential (PAT or token) the autolander service uses to call `workflow_dispatch` on `gerrit-verify.yaml`, what scope is required (`Actions: read+write`), and how it is provisioned to the autolander process (distinct from g2p's Gerrit-container-scoped PAT).
findings.finding: S7 must dispatch `gerrit-verify.yaml` directly via the GitHub Actions `workflow_dispatch` API from the autolander service, but the plan does not state what credential the autolander uses for this call, nor how it is provisioned. The existing PAT for this purpose is materialised only into the Gerrit container's g2p config (SSM → `gerrit_to_platform.ini`) — the autolander is a separate service and cannot reuse that materialised credential without explicit provisioning steps.
findings.location: AC2: 'dispatching gerrit-verify.yaml against the real combined-tip change's actual Change-Id / change-number / patchset'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an auth-preconditions note to AC2: e.g. 'The autolander service uses a fine-grained PAT with Actions:read+write on navapbc/rebar, stored in SSM at `/rebar/prod/autolander-github-pat` and injected at boot — same provisioning pattern as g2p's PAT (runbook §1).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike or RED fixture that assembles two minimal independent relation chains into a single combined chain on a test Gerrit instance (or a mock), verifying the combined tip's Change-Id, patchset, and parent lineage are correct before committing to the full design.
findings.finding: The cross-stack linear chain assembly — combining N independent Gerrit relation chains into one combined chain via `/rebase:chain` — is a novel pattern with no bors/Graphite precedent on Gerrit, and no spike, RED fixture, or prototype is planned to verify it works under Gerrit's relation-chain semantics before full build-out.
findings.location: What section; Acceptance Criteria — 'Assembles queued changes/stacks into one combined linear chain' AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `phase`/`status` field (with explicit enum vocabulary, e.g. `{assembling, ci_pending, ci_green, handed_off, failed}`) to the S7 batch handoff record schema so S8 can safely gate on `ci_green` before casting any vote.
findings.finding: The batch handoff record schema {member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id} has no phase/status field and no lifecycle state-machine enumeration for the batch object itself. S8 must read this record to cast votes, but cannot distinguish a 'ready-for-S8' record from an 'assembly-in-progress' or 'CI-pending' record. Contrast with S2's wipChain which explicitly enumerates phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle}.
findings.location: Verified-satisfaction section; AC4 — handoff record definition
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the handoff record to: {member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id, phase} where phase ∈ {assembling, ci_pending, ci_green, handed_off, failed}. S8 must only act on records in phase=ci_green.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the handoff record write is atomic and idempotent (e.g., upsert keyed on `combined_tip_change_id`), and define the recovery path when the write fails after a successful CI dispatch (e.g., re-check for an existing in-flight run_id before re-dispatching).
findings.finding: Partial-state gap: if S7 successfully dispatches CI (run_id obtained) but then crashes or errors before writing the handoff record, the CI run is orphaned and S8 has no record to read. The plan does not specify idempotency for the handoff record write or a recovery path for this window.
findings.location: AC line 4 — 'hand off to S8 via the handoff record {member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id}'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the handoff record is written and read atomically (e.g., via a versioned upsert / compare-and-swap, or by including a monotonic version field that S8 validates has not changed between its read and its vote-cast), so a concurrent re-batch cannot corrupt S8's view.
findings.finding: Concurrency ownership gap on the handoff record during re-batching: if S7 executes a re-batch (overwriting the handoff record with a new combined_tip_change_id and run_id) while S8 has already started reading the previous record, the plan provides no locking, CAS, or versioning to prevent S8 from acting on a record that S7 is simultaneously replacing.
findings.location: AC line 3 (re-batch loop) cross-referenced with AC line 4 (handoff record) and 'Verified-satisfaction' section.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a version/generation field to the handoff record; S8 reads (record, version) at start and re-reads (or uses an optimistic lock) before casting each vote, abandoning if the version changed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete storage medium (e.g., `infra/autolander/batch_state.json`, an in-process queue, a DB table) and module path where S7 writes and S8 reads the handoff record `{member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id}`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the handoff record `{member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id}` is written by S7 and read by S8 before casting any vote — but no storage medium, schema definition, file path, or API for this record exists anywhere in the repository.
findings.location: Verified-satisfaction section; AC line 4 ('hand off to S8 via the handoff record')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `file_impact` entry such as `infra/autolander/batch_state.py` defining the schema as a dataclass/TypedDict, and reference it explicitly in both S7's and S8's ACs so the contract is grounded in a real artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the re-batch attempt counter lives (e.g. as a `rebatch_attempts` field on the batch record or a named in-memory variable), and define how it is initialized, incremented, and reset, so the N=5 bound survives restarts.
findings.finding: The re-batch attempt counter (bounded to N=5) is described only in AC3 prose but is never assigned to any schema field, in-memory variable, or named state store. Its placement is fully ambiguous — an implementer cannot determine whether it is a field on the handoff record, a local variable in the assembly loop, or a separate persistent counter.
findings.location: AC3 — FFO TOCTOU re-check

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the fallback for `workflow_dispatch` failure in AC2: specify at minimum a retry policy (e.g. N retries with backoff) and what happens to the batch if dispatch cannot succeed after retries (drop to serial? discard batch? alarm?).
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the sub-step where CI dispatch (`workflow_dispatch` of `gerrit-verify.yaml`) fails. AC2 mandates recording `run_id` on the batch, but if the dispatch call fails (network error, GHA API outage, rate-limit, invalid inputs), the plan provides no error path — the batch is left in an undefined state with no run_id.
findings.location: AC2 — CI trigger / run_id recording

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC (e.g., for AC2: `gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs --jq '[.workflow_runs[] | select(.name=="gerrit-verify")] | length' | grep -E '^1$'`; for AC4: `jq -e '.run_id and .tested_sha and (.member_change_ids | length > 0)' batch-state.json`; for AC5: inject 9 changes into the assembler and assert only 8 are selected).
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has a concrete proving command or verification step. The plan contains only behavioral claims ('assembles', 'triggers exactly ONE', 'hands off') with no test commands, scripts, or deterministic checks that would produce evidence of satisfaction. This makes done-ness unverifiable and leaves a gap between 'what we'll do' and 'what done looks like'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five ACs

findings.finding: No proving commands or implementation steps are described for any of the four ACs; the plan has only a Why/What/Scope/AC structure with zero verification commands, so an executor cannot produce evidence of doneness without inventing the verification approach from scratch.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs)
findings.suggested_fix:
For AC2: `gh run list --workflow=ci.yml --branch=<batch-tip> --json databaseId | jq -e '[.[] | select(.status=="completed")] | length == 1'`
For AC3: assert `git rev-parse <batch-tip>` equals the SHA of the submitted commit on main.
For AC1: `git log --oneline main..<batch-tip> | wc -l` equals the expected stack depth.
For AC4: add a config constant (e.g., `MAX_BATCH_JOBS=N`) and assert it in a unit test.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the BATCH_MAX=8 justification: either (a) clarify that the per-change concurrency group is *also* the per-run job budget limit and they point to the same number, or (b) replace AC5's parenthetical with the actual bounding constraint (e.g. the per-change concurrency group size or a measured job-slot limit), so Why and AC5 reference the same limiter.
findings.finding: The 'Why' section explicitly states 'the exact concurrent-run count isn't the limiter — gerrit-verify.yaml uses a per-change concurrency group,' yet AC5 justifies BATCH_MAX=8 as 'sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap' — the very cap Why says is not the limiter. An implementer reading both sections gets contradictory signals about what actually bounds BATCH_MAX.
findings.location: Why section vs. AC5
findings.suggested_fix: In AC5, replace '(sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap)' with '(sized to the per-change concurrency-group slot limit in gerrit-verify.yaml, consistent with the Why rationale)' — or, if the job budget is a distinct secondary constraint, name it explicitly and explain why it is more binding than the concurrency group.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile BATCH_MAX=8's sizing rationale: either cite the actual binding constraint (a measured GHA runner job-slot limit, Gerrit relation-chain depth cap, or similar) and remove the contradiction with the 'Why' section's statement that the concurrency cap is not the limiter, or — if no such measurement exists — acknowledge the number is a conservative placeholder and document how it should be tuned.
findings.finding: BATCH_MAX=8 is a premature-optimization / unjustified constant: the plan's own 'Why' section explicitly states the concurrent-run count is NOT the limiter ('gerrit-verify.yaml uses a per-change concurrency group'), yet AC5 justifies BATCH_MAX=8 as 'sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap' — the very cap that Why disavows as the governing constraint. The codebase confirms the per-Change-Id concurrency group (gerrit-verify.yaml lines 40-43), which does not cross-cancel between changes. With no profiling result, no measured job-slot limit, and an internal contradiction in the rationale, the constant is an optimization whose basis cannot be verified.
findings.location: Why section vs. AC5 (BATCH_MAX=8 sizing rationale)
findings.suggested_fix: In AC5, replace '(sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap)' with the actual constraint, e.g., '(conservative placeholder; sized against [specific limit, e.g., Gerrit relation-chain API max depth or measured runner memory]; tune by monitoring [observable metric])'. Update Why to be consistent, or remove the per-change concurrency-group caveat if it is not load-bearing for the batch-size decision.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between 'concurrent-run count isn't the limiter' (Why section) and 'sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap' (AC5) by providing a single, coherent positive rationale for BATCH_MAX=8 (e.g., operational blast-radius on batch failure, Gerrit REST rate limits, rebase latency budget, or empirical queue depth).
findings.finding: The BATCH_MAX=8 justification is internally self-contradicting: the 'Why' section explicitly states 'the exact concurrent-run count isn't the limiter — `gerrit-verify.yaml` uses a per-change concurrency group,' yet AC5 justifies BATCH_MAX=8 as 'sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap.' If the concurrency cap is not the limiter, the stated rationale for the specific numeric cap of 8 is unsupported. The plan provides no alternative grounding (e.g., Gerrit REST throughput, rebase latency, wall-clock SLA) for why 8 specifically — rather than 4 or 16 — is correct.
findings.location: AC (bullet 5): 'Batch size bounded by a concrete numeric cap `BATCH_MAX=8`... sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap'; cross-ref 'Why' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the self-contradiction between 'the exact concurrent-run count isn't the limiter' (Why) and 'sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap' (AC5) by identifying the actual constraint that bounds BATCH_MAX and deriving the 8-member cap from it (e.g., rebase time budget per batch cycle, Gerrit API call volume, or an explicit policy choice).
findings.finding: BATCH_MAX=8 is internally contradictory: the Why section states 'the exact concurrent-run count isn't the limiter — gerrit-verify.yaml uses a per-change concurrency group,' yet AC5 justifies BATCH_MAX=8 as 'sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap.' If the concurrency cap is not the limiter, the sizing rationale for BATCH_MAX collapses, leaving the cap an unverified/unjustified numeric assertion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 ('Batch size bounded by a concrete numeric cap BATCH_MAX=8'); Why section ('the exact concurrent-run count isn't the limiter')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise the story title to reflect that S7 delivers assembly + CI trigger only (not landing), e.g. 'S7 Cross-stack batching: combined-chain assembly + single CI trigger (Phase 2)'.
findings.finding: The title phrase 'land together' overpromises relative to the plan body: this story explicitly does NOT submit changes (landing is deferred to S8/S9). A reader scanning the backlog will expect atomic submission to be part of S7's deliverable.
findings.location: Story title: 'S7 Cross-stack batching: combined-chain CI on tip, land together (Phase 2)'
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'S7 Cross-stack batching: combined-chain assembly + single CI trigger (Phase 2)' — reserving 'land together' for the S8/S9 summary or an epic-level description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the file(s) S7 creates or modifies for its batch assembly logic (e.g., `infra/autolander/batch.py` or an extension to `loop.py`) and specify where the handoff record is written so S8's consumer has a concrete read target.
findings.finding: S7 introduces substantial new behavior (batch assembly, bounded re-batch loop, BATCH_MAX=8 cap enforcement, handoff record writer) but names NO file or module as the edit target for this logic. The dependency `infra/autolander/` does not exist in the repo (S2 is still open/unmerged), and S7 adds no `file_impact` or equivalent naming of where the batch code lives.
findings.location: Scope section; Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs); Dependencies section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `file_impact` section (or equivalent in Scope) listing at minimum: `infra/autolander/batch.py` (batch assembly + re-batch loop + BATCH_MAX cap) and the handoff record's storage location (in-memory dict? persisted file? named key in a shared state store?). This gives S8 a concrete path to read from.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a measurement plan (e.g., a fixture or dry-run probe against GHA free-tier job slots) to derive or validate BATCH_MAX=8 and N=5 before hardcoding them, or explicitly mark them as provisional constants to be tuned after first production batch.
findings.finding: BATCH_MAX=8 is asserted ('sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap') without any measurement, derivation, or plan to validate it; similarly N=5 re-batch attempts is an ungrounded heuristic. Both are thresholds whose correctness cannot be established by reasoning alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — BATCH_MAX=8 bullet; FFO TOCTOU re-check bullet (N=5)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or implementation note: 'BATCH_MAX and MAX_REBATCH_ATTEMPTS are read from config (env/YAML) and default to 8/5; these defaults are validated by [spike/fixture name] before Phase-2 launch.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expose BATCH_MAX and MAX_REBATCH_ATTEMPTS as named, documented configuration values (env vars or config file keys) rather than inline literals, with the defaults set to 8 and 5 respectively.
findings.finding: BATCH_MAX=8 and N=5 (MAX_REBATCH_ATTEMPTS) read as hardcoded constants rather than configurable values, yet both are thresholds explicitly tied to external conditions (GHA concurrency cap, branch contention rate) that are expected to evolve.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — BATCH_MAX=8 bullet; FFO TOCTOU re-check bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to implementation: `BATCH_MAX = int(os.getenv('AUTOMERGE_BATCH_MAX', '8'))` and `MAX_REBATCH = int(os.getenv('AUTOMERGE_MAX_REBATCH', '5'))`, documented in CLAUDE.md or a config reference.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the cross-stack batching architecture: the Gerrit adaptation rationale, the S7→S8 handoff contract schema, and the BATCH_MAX/N=5 derivation (or provisional status).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — adapting bors/Graphite-style cross-stack batching to Gerrit relation chains, including a new inter-story handoff contract (S7→S8 record schema) — with no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry planned.
findings.location: What section; Verified-satisfaction section; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: S7 must emit a structured log entry (and ideally a metric/counter) for each key failure event — re-batch limit exhausted, CI dispatch failure, chain assembly error — with fields sufficient for debugging (batch_id, member_change_ids, attempt_count, error).
findings.finding: No observability is specified for S7's failure paths: when the re-batch limit (N=5) is hit and the batch is dropped to serial, when CI dispatch fails, or when chain assembly fails mid-way. Without instrumentation, operators cannot detect or debug stalls.
findings.location: AC line 3 — 'bounded to N=5 re-batch attempts, after which the batch is dropped to the Phase-1 serial path'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE path for the handoff record: specify when it is deleted or marked as consumed (e.g., after S8 successfully submits or explicitly abandons the batch), and who is responsible (S8 on completion, or a separate GC step).
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is defined for handoff records: after S8 consumes a record and submits (or abandons) the batch, the record is never described as being deleted or archived. Records will accumulate indefinitely.
findings.location: Section 'Verified-satisfaction' — describes handoff record structure; no retirement path mentioned anywhere in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or note: 'S8 marks the handoff record as consumed (status=submitted|abandoned) upon completion; a periodic GC job purges records older than T days to prevent unbounded accumulation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test coverage (unit or integration) for the three new flows introduced by S7: (a) the bounded re-batch loop — assert fallback to serial on the 5th consecutive re-batch; (b) BATCH_MAX=8 enforcement — assert the 9th candidate is deferred, not included; (c) handoff-record integrity — assert the written `{member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id}` matches the actual assembled chain and the triggered run_id.
findings.finding: The plan introduces at least three new user-facing / system-observable flows — (1) the bounded re-batch loop (up to N=5 re-assemble cycles, then fall back to serial), (2) the BATCH_MAX=8 cap with overflow queuing, and (3) the handoff-record assembly `{member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id}` — but specifies NO testing work for any of them: no unit tests, no integration tests, no test ACs, no test-authoring tasks. Failure/timeout/boundary paths (main advances repeatedly; batch size exactly at and one above BATCH_MAX; conflicting changes in candidate set; CI trigger failure; empty queue) are entirely unaddressed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs); 'Scope' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Testing' section (or test-authoring ACs) covering at minimum: (1) re-batch loop unit test: mock main advancing N times, assert fallback fires on attempt 6; (2) BATCH_MAX boundary test: feed 9 candidates, assert batch size == 8 and 1 deferred; (3) handoff-record contract test: assert each field is populated from the actual assembled change, not from stale pre-rebase state; (4) conflict-during-assembly test: feed two conflicting changes, assert neither is included in the batch (or the batch is aborted cleanly).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that S2 (`infra/autolander/loop.py`) is implemented and merged before S7 begins, or explicitly scope S7 to implement the selection/rebase/`is:submittable` primitives itself rather than asserting reuse of a pre-manifest module.
findings.finding: The plan asserts S7 'reuses S2's selection, rebase, and `is:submittable` machinery' as an already-available building block, but S2's sole implementation file (`infra/autolander/loop.py`) does not exist in the repository — S2 is `open`/`pre-manifest`. The reuse claim rests on an unimplemented dependency.
findings.location: Dependencies section; AC line 1 ('reusing S2's `/rebase:chain` as the building block')
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit `depends_on` to the S2 ticket at the story level and note in Scope that S7 cannot begin until S2's `loop.py` is merged; alternatively, if S7 is intended to be developed in parallel, enumerate the specific API boundaries (function signatures / module paths) S7 will import from S2 so the interface contract is concrete.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope section that S7 CREATES a new batch assembly module (not merely extends existing code), and gate S7's start on S2 being merged so the 'reuse' claim is grounded in landed code.
findings.finding: All five Acceptance Criteria describe net-new behavior (CREATION), but the plan does not classify them as out-of-scope new behavior or acknowledge that no prior story has delivered the batch assembly infrastructure S7 extends. This leaves the behavioral scope boundary ambiguous between 'extending S2' and 'creating a new subsystem'.
findings.location: Scope section; all ACs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the storage mechanism and location for the handoff record (e.g., in-process shared state, a named file in `infra/autolander/`, a queue entry) so S8's consumer has a concrete, unambiguous read target.
findings.finding: S8 is named as the consumer of the handoff record `{member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id}`, but no file path, module, or interface for S8 is enumerated. As an external consumer of S7's output, S8's read-side contract is unspecified, making S8 an unenumerated consumer with no grounded edit target.
findings.location: Verified-satisfaction section; AC4 ('hand off to S8')
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Verified-satisfaction section: 'S7 persists the handoff record to [e.g., `infra/autolander/batch_state.json` / an in-process dict keyed by batch_id / a named SSM parameter] which S8 reads.' Then reference that location in AC4.

findings.finding: The plan's 'ancestor-atomic submit' mechanism has a hidden FFO TOCTOU race: under Fast Forward Only (project.config `action = fast forward only`, ADR-0040), the entire combined chain becomes non-submittable the instant any other change lands on `main` between CI completion and the submit call. The plan presents atomic submit as a terminal step with no acknowledgement of this race, no retry loop, and no fallback — yet this race is the dominant failure mode under any non-trivial landing cadence (ADR-0040 §Consequences: 'median inter-landing gap ≈ CI duration; ~46% of landings overlap a prior CI run').
findings.location: AC3 ('On green, submits the whole batch atomically (ancestor-atomic submit); the tested tip == the landed tree')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What/AC: 'If main advances between CI completion and submit, the batch is discarded and each stack is returned to the Autosubmit queue for the next batch cycle (or Phase 1 serial fallback is invoked), rather than attempting a rebase-and-resubmit of the full batch.'

findings.finding: The plan invokes 'Graphite/bors stack-aware model on Gerrit relation chains' as the feasibility precedent for the combined-chain atomic submit, but Graphite and bors are GitHub-PR-native tools with no Gerrit API surface. The analogy is asserted but does not constitute a verified proof that Gerrit's relation-chain submit semantics support the same batching pattern, particularly given that ADR-0040 explicitly documents a Gerrit FFO + submitWholeTopic limitation for parallel sibling changes.
findings.location: What section: 'Graphite/bors stack-aware model on Gerrit relation chains'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
